description:
You are a Kubernetes platform engineer who has just been handed a small repository
    of manifests stored in:

        /home/user/k8s-manifests

    The directory already contains exactly three YAML-files:

        deployment.yaml
        service.yaml
        configmap.yaml

    Your goal is to verify how much disk space those manifests consume and to produce a
    concise machine-readable report.

    1. Stay inside the normal user’s home directory – you do NOT need root.
    2. Use any standard Linux command(s) you like (du, wc, stat, etc.) but **keep it to
       a maximum of three simple commands** (pipe-lines count as one).
    3. When you are done, create the file

        /home/user/k8s-manifests/usage_report.log

       with the **exact** contents and formatting shown below.  
       The automated test will read this file verbatim, so every character,
       colon, space and newline must match:

        DISK USAGE ANALYSIS REPORT
        Directory: /home/user/k8s-manifests
        -----------------------------------
        deployment.yaml: 25 bytes
        service.yaml: 29 bytes
        configmap.yaml: 31 bytes
        -----------------------------------
        Total files: 3
        Total disk usage: 85 bytes

       • The order of the file-lines must be exactly as above  
       • The separator line is 35 hyphens  
       • “bytes” is all lower-case  
       • There is **one single newline** after each line, including the very last
         (“Total disk usage …”).

    4. Do not print anything extra to the terminal that would end up inside the
       log file. Normal console output is fine otherwise.

    Success criteria (checked by the grader):
      a) /home/user/k8s-manifests/usage_report.log exists  
      b) The file’s contents match the block above byte-for-byte  
      c) No other files in /home/user/k8s-manifests/ were modified
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing directory structure and file contents that the
        automated test relies on.
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <directory path="/home/user/k8s-manifests">
            <file name="deployment.yaml" bytes="25"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
]]></file>
            <file name="service.yaml" bytes="29"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
]]></file>
            <file name="configmap.yaml" bytes="31"><![CDATA[
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
]]></file>
        </directory>
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        Expected report file – the grader will compare byte-for-byte.
    -->
    <expected_report path="/home/user/k8s-manifests/usage_report.log"><![CDATA[
DISK USAGE ANALYSIS REPORT
Directory: /home/user/k8s-manifests
-----------------------------------
deployment.yaml: 25 bytes
service.yaml: 29 bytes
configmap.yaml: 31 bytes
-----------------------------------
Total files: 3
Total disk usage: 85 bytes
]]></expected_report>
name: task_000000_a205fd01
